title: Add a Robot Using the Robot Selection Screen
summary: Use the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen to add a robot by entering the corresponding robot ID, clicking Add, and confirming the system reports that the add was successful.
when_to_use: Use when a robot needs to be added through the Robot Selection Add and Remove interface and the operator has the corresponding robot ID.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen, enter the corresponding robot ID, click Add, and verify the system shows the add was successful.

access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen

access_required: Access to the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Robot Selection Add and Remove interface with Add Robot instructions and success confirmation language.

steps.title: Open or locate the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen
steps.purpose: Ensure the operator is on the correct interface before attempting to add a robot.
steps.instruction: Open or locate the "Robot Selection Add and Remove" screen.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection Add and Remove screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen title and Add Robot section are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The Robot Selection Add and Remove screen cannot be found or the Add Robot section is not visible.

actions.label: Open Robot Selection screen
actions.exact_action: Open or locate the "Robot Selection Add and Remove" screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen title "Robot Selection Add and Remove" and the Add Robot area with robot ID entry and Add button.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen is not available or cannot be located.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide title appears as "Robot Selection Add and Remove" with sections for Add Robot and Remove Robot.

steps.title: Enter the corresponding robot ID
steps.purpose: Provide the identifier the system uses to add the intended robot.
steps.instruction: In the Add Robot section, enter the corresponding robot ID.
steps.expected_result: The robot ID is entered in the Add Robot input field.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended robot ID is present in the Add Robot field.
steps.failure_condition: The robot ID cannot be entered or the correct robot ID is not available.

actions.label: Enter robot ID
actions.exact_action: In the Add Robot section, enter the corresponding robot ID.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Robot section and robot ID entry field.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the corresponding robot ID is not available.
Stop and escalate if the robot ID field does not accept input.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text for Add Robot states: "1. Enter the corresponding robot ID 2. Click 'Add' 3. The system will show that the adding was successful."
OCR text states: "Enter the corresponding robot ID".

steps.title: Click Add
steps.purpose: Submit the add request for the entered robot ID.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the add request.
steps.healthy_condition: The Add action is accepted by the interface.
steps.failure_condition: The Add action cannot be selected or the system does not process the request.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add button in the Add Robot section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the Add button is unavailable or does not respond.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text for Add Robot states: "1. Enter the corresponding robot ID 2. Click 'Add' 3. The system will show that the adding was successful."

steps.title: Verify the add was successful
steps.purpose: Confirm the system accepted the add action.
steps.instruction: Verify that the system shows that the adding was successful.
steps.expected_result: The system shows a success indication for the add action.
steps.healthy_condition: A success indication confirms the robot was added successfully.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not show that the adding was successful.

actions.label: Confirm success indication
actions.exact_action: Verify that the system shows that the adding was successful.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Success indication associated with the Add Robot action.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the system does not show that the adding was successful, because the source does not provide additional recovery steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text for Add Robot states: "1. Enter the corresponding robot ID 2. Click 'Add' 3. The system will show that the adding was successful."
OCR text states the system will show that adding was successful.

success_criteria: The corresponding robot ID is entered in the Add Robot section.
The system shows that the adding was successful.
healthy_conditions: The Robot Selection Add and Remove screen is available.
The Add Robot section accepts the corresponding robot ID.
The system provides a success indication after the Add action.
failure_conditions: The Robot Selection Add and Remove screen cannot be located.
The corresponding robot ID cannot be entered.
The Add action cannot be completed.
The system does not show that the adding was successful.
escalation_guidance: If the system does not show that the adding was successful, stop and escalate because the source does not provide additional recovery steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection Add and Remove screen, Add Robot section, robot ID field, Add button, and success indication area.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: OCR text for Add Robot states: "1. Enter the corresponding robot ID 2. Click 'Add' 3. The system will show that the adding was successful."

missing_details: The source segment does not provide explicit navigation steps for reaching the Robot Selection Add and Remove screen.
The source does not define when to add a robot versus when not to use this procedure.
The source does not specify the exact wording or location of the success indication.
The source does not provide recovery steps if the add action fails.
The source does not provide role boundaries beyond basic operator use.
The source does not provide time estimates, production stop requirements, or LOTO requirements.
